nextFocus: Complete the next shim/daemon usability batch, including tighter CLI and shim behavior coverage for supported non-interactive execution paths.
Expand integration tests so the runspace, shim resolution, policy denial, and session flow are exercised more like real usage.
Tighten the user-facing CLI and getting-started documentation anywhere it still implies broader shell replacement than is actually supported.
remainingGaps: The product still does not offer a true agentsh-style drop-in PowerShell replacement with interactive shell interception.
Linux and macOS enforcement remain mostly structural abstractions rather than verified runtime controls.
Daemon and shim behavior are still not exercised through a deeper external-process end-to-end workflow.
Network enforcement is still pre-execution intent filtering rather than full OS-level network blocking.
Cross-platform packaging and runtime verification remain behind the repository's architectural ambition.
strengths: The repository now describes its verified runtime surface more honestly, which reduces the gap between user expectation and actual behavior.
Unit and integration coverage around the current command-execution path improved, including real checks for hosted PowerShell and CLI-level network denial.
The current CLI/session/policy/reporting baseline remains solid and tested after the documentation and integration hardening pass.
